system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

vertices

    (1 0 0.1)

    (0 1 0.1)

);

edges
(
);

boundary
(

        faces

            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces

            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }

    {

        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
            (3 7 6 2)
        );

        (
            (4 5 6 7)
        );

    {

        (

        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     shallowWaterFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         100;

deltaT          0.1;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   1;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression uncompressed;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,hU)     Gauss linearUpwind grad(hU);
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    h
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-12;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    hFinal
    {
        $h;
        relTol          0;
    }

    hU
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-12;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    hUFinal
    {
        $hU;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    3;
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    momentumPredictor   yes;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='setFieldsDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      setFieldsDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

defaultFieldValues
(
    volScalarFieldValue h 0.02
    volVectorFieldValue hU (0.001 0 0)
);

regions
(
    boxToCell
    {
        box (0.4 0.4 -1) (0.6 0.6 1);
        fieldValues
        (
            volScalarFieldValue h 0.02
            volVectorFieldValue hU (0.001 0 0)
        );
    }
);

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.shallowWaterFoam', 'error_content': "ERROR: 
Expected a '(' while reading VectorSpace<Form, Cmpt, Ncmpts>, found on line 10 the doubleScalar 9.81

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run1/runs/shallowWaterWithSquareBump/2/constant/gravitationalProperties/g at line 10.

    From function Foam::Istream& Foam::Istream::readBegin(const char*)
    in file db/IOstreams/IOstreams/Istream.C at line 92.

FOAM exiting"}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
Expected a '(' while reading VectorSpace<Form, Cmpt, Ncmpts>, found on line 10 the doubleScalar 9.81
```

This means `shallowWaterFoam` expects `g` to be a **vector** (since it reads it as a `VectorSpace`), but the current `gravitationalProperties` file defines it as a **scalar** value `9.81`.

In the `squareBump` reference case (which this is based on), `gravitationalProperties` should define `g` as a dimensioned vector, not a scalar.

## Root Cause

The `constant/gravitationalProperties` file has:

```
g               g [ 0 1 -2 0 0 0 0 ] 9.81;
```

However, `shallowWaterFoam` expects `g` to be a **dimensioned vector**, e.g.:

```
g               g [ 0 1 -2 0 0 0 0 ] (0 9.81 0);
```

The solver reads `g` as a vector quantity (gravity direction/magnitude), not a scalar.

## Proposed Solution

**Modify `constant/gravitationalProperties`:**

Change the gravity entry from a scalar:
```
g               g [ 0 1 -2 0 0 0 0 ] 9.81;
```

to a vector:
```
g               g [ 0 1 -2 0 0 0 0 ] (0 9.81 0);
```

This matches the expected format for `shallowWaterFoam`, where `g` is read as a `dimensionedVector`. The squareBump reference case uses `(0 -9.81 0)` depending on the coordinate system convention — verify the sign convention in the reference case if needed. Since the user requires flow in the **+x direction** (momentum `(0.001, 0, 0)`), ensure the gravity vector aligns with the coordinate system such that the flow develops correctly in the intended direction after initialization. In most shallow water implementations, gravity acts in the negative z-direction, so `(0 -9.81 0)` may be correct — check the reference case `constant/gravitationalProperties` for the exact format used there.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'constant/gravitationalProperties', 'changes': 'Change g from scalar to vector format: replace g g [ 0 1 -2 0 0 0 0 ] 9.81 with g g [ 0 1 -2 0 0 0 0 ] (0 9.81 0)'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a shallow water simulation over a square bump using the shallowWaterFoam solver. The domain spans 1 x 1 x 0.1, discretized into 20 x 20 x 1 grid points. The simulation initializes with a uniform water depth of 0.02 m and a uniform momentum field of (0.001,0,0). The simulation runs from 0 s to 100 s with a timestep of 0.1 s and writes results every 1 s. </user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
